conflicts.description: GDPR compliance requirement conflicts with strict India-only data residency constraint

version_a.text: The platform must comply with GDPR regulations to support NRI customers. It must also comply with the Indian IT Act.

version_b.text: All customer data must be stored exclusively on servers located within India. No customer data may be stored or processed outside India.

conflicts.description: ZestMoney is listed as a required in-scope integration in TR-005 but TR-033 and TR-006 classify it as contingent and potentially post-launch, creating ambiguity about launch scope

version_a.text: The platform must support credit card EMI and no-cost EMI through financing partners including Bajaj Finance and ZestMoney.

version_b.text: ZestMoney integration will be included in scope but may be delivered post-launch pending commercial agreement finalization.

conflicts.description: TR-018 and US-003 define product search by name, category, and brand, but TR-019 and US-004 add filtering by price range, ratings, and delivery time — the search and filter boundary is inconsistently defined across requirements, with US-003 partially duplicating TR-018 scope

version_a.text: The platform must provide a product search capability allowing customers to search by product name, category, and brand.

version_b.text: The system shall allow customers to search for products by name, brand, or category so that they can quickly locate desired products.

conflicts.description: TR-026 requires handling 50,000 concurrent users as a fixed minimum, while TR-039 requires elastic scaling to handle sudden spikes without specifying an upper bound — these may imply different architectural approaches and cost profiles that need alignment

version_a.text: The platform must handle a minimum of 50,000 concurrent users to support peak traffic during sale events such as Diwali.

version_b.text: The platform must be designed to scale elastically to handle sudden traffic spikes during promotional sale events without performance degradation.

conflicts.description: US-011 restricts review submission to customers who have received a delivered product, while TR-020 states customers must be able to submit reviews without specifying a purchase or delivery precondition, creating inconsistency in eligibility rules

version_a.text: Customers must be able to submit product reviews and ratings. All reviews must go through a moderation workflow before being published.

version_b.text: The system shall allow customers who have received a delivered product to submit a written review and star rating so that only verified purchasers can review products.
